ctx.name: Carlos
ctx.notes: For Carlos, this performance conversation needs to make the quality bar concrete and usable. I want to understand whether prior feedback translated into behavior changes or stayed abstract. I also need to hear how he defines good work right now and where he feels blocked in growth. Without that shared baseline, feedback can feel subjective and demotivating. My goal is to align on specific expectations, examples, and one near-term development focus he can practice immediately.

focusPoints.label: Making the quality bar concrete with examples
focusPoints.reason: What “good” looks like in practice for Carlos right now, using concrete examples so feedback doesn’t stay abstract.

focusPoints.label: Whether prior feedback changed his behavior
focusPoints.reason: What prior feedback he actually translated into new habits or outputs, and where it stayed at the level of ideas.

focusPoints.label: His definition of good work at this level
focusPoints.reason: What he thinks the bar is for his junior role, so you can calibrate expectations against his current mental model.

focusPoints.label: Near-term growth focus and what blocks it
focusPoints.reason: How to turn growth into one specific practice for the next cycle, and what feels blocked so the plan is actionable.

transcript.question: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
transcript.answer: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.

transcript.question: name wins, always — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
transcript.answer: Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.

transcript.question: Under time pressure, what specifically starts to slip in your work?
transcript.answer: I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.

transcript.question: Which recent piece of work best shows your current quality bar?
transcript.answer: I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.

transcript.question: want clearer examples — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
transcript.answer: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.

transcript.question: reviews broad, sure — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
transcript.answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.

transcript.question: Where do you think your work most often misses the bar?
transcript.answer: A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.

transcript.question: How do you decide when a design is ready to ship?
transcript.answer: If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.

transcript.question: What would you like to have changed by our next check-in?
transcript.answer: I will start by writing one explicit priority rule I will use when time pressure hits this month.
